system.name: canasta-familiar
system.description: B2C digital platform for selling grocery and household staple products. Customers browse a catalog, place orders online, and receive home delivery using the company's own courier fleet. Supports card payments via an external payment gateway and cash-on-delivery.

systemDecisions.title: System profile
systemDecisions.rationale: Confirms how much domain design investment the system deserves.
systemDecisions.prompt: Review the DSL design decision "System profile". Context: canasta-familiar (unclassified) Relevant files: arch/system/system.yaml. Task: Evaluate the available options (core | supporting | generic) and update the design only if the current decision should change. Keep the artifacts technology-agnostic and run the DSL validation/refinement checks after proposing changes.

systemDecisions.title: Bounded context boundaries
systemDecisions.current: 7 BC(s), type split: core: 2, supporting: 4, generic: 1
systemDecisions.rationale: BC boundaries drive ownership, language and downstream generation modules.
systemDecisions.prompt: Review the DSL design decision "Bounded context boundaries". Context: 7 BC(s), type split: core: 2, supporting: 4, generic: 1 Relevant files: arch/system/system.yaml. Task: Evaluate the available options (keep current BCs | split oversized BCs | merge thin BCs | reclassify core/supporting/generic) and update the design only if the current decision should change. Keep the artifacts technology-agnostic and run the DSL validation/refinement checks after proposing changes.

systemDecisions.title: Integration style
systemDecisions.current: 16 integration(s), patterns: event: 13, customer-supplier: 1, acl: 2, channels: message-broker: 13, http: 3
systemDecisions.rationale: This is the main coupling and availability map of the system.
systemDecisions.prompt: Review the DSL design decision "Integration style". Context: 16 integration(s), patterns: event: 13, customer-supplier: 1, acl: 2, channels: message-broker: 13, http: 3 Relevant files: arch/system/system.yaml. Task: Evaluate the available options (customer-supplier http | event message-broker | acl | open-host | shared-kernel) and update the design only if the current decision should change. Keep the artifacts technology-agnostic and run the DSL validation/refinement checks after proposing changes.

systemDecisions.title: Infrastructure constraints
systemDecisions.current: deployment=modular-monolith, database=relational, authServer=true, messageBroker=true, objectStorage=1 bucket(s) (product-media: public/public-url)
systemDecisions.rationale: Infrastructure flags explain why generated projects need shared auth, broker, or object storage capabilities.
systemDecisions.prompt: Review the DSL design decision "Infrastructure constraints". Context: deployment=modular-monolith, database=relational, authServer=true, messageBroker=true, objectStorage=1 bucket(s) (product-media: public/public-url) Relevant files: arch/system/system.yaml. Task: Evaluate the available options (modular-monolith | microservices | schema-per-bc | db-per-bc | auth server on/off | message broker on/off | object storage on/off) and update the design only if the current decision should change. Keep the artifacts technology-agnostic and run the DSL validation/refinement checks after proposing changes.

systemDecisions.title: External systems and sagas
systemDecisions.rationale: External dependencies and cross-BC processes are high-value review points before generation.
systemDecisions.prompt: Review the DSL design decision "External systems and sagas". Context: 2 external system(s), 1 saga(s), 3 actor type(s) Relevant files: arch/system/system.yaml. Task: Evaluate the available options (declare external ACL | model saga | keep process inside one BC | declare actors for validation) and update the design only if the current decision should change. Keep the artifacts technology-agnostic and run the DSL validation/refinement checks after proposing changes.

systemDecisions.title: Reliability patterns
systemDecisions.rationale: Event reliability flags control at-least-once publication and consumer deduplication; recommended on whenever sagas exist.
systemDecisions.prompt: Review the DSL design decision "Reliability patterns". Context: outbox=true (retention 7d), consumerIdempotency=true (retention 14d) Relevant files: arch/system/system.yaml. Task: Evaluate the available options (outbox on/off | consumerIdempotency on/off | set retention days) and update the design only if the current decision should change. Keep the artifacts technology-agnostic and run the DSL validation/refinement checks after proposing changes.

boundedContexts.name: catalog
boundedContexts.purpose: Manages the product catalog lifecycle: categories, products, images, and pricing. Single authoritative source for all product data and prices in the platform.

ruleDetails.description: Category name must be unique across all categories.
Category name must be unique across all categories.
A category cannot be deactivated if it has ACTIVE products assigned to it. All active products must be reassigned or discontinued before deactivation.
SKU must be unique across all products in the catalog.
A product can only be assigned to a category in ACTIVE status. Assigning a product to an INACTIVE category is not permitted.
SKU must be unique across all products in the catalog.
A DISCONTINUED product is in a terminal state and cannot be modified. Any attempt to update, add images, or change the status of a discontinued product must be rejected.
A product can only be assigned to a category in ACTIVE status. Assigning a product to an INACTIVE category is not permitted.

A product must have a non-empty name, a positive price, and be assigned to a category with ACTIVE status before it can be activated. If the category is INACTIVE, activation is blocked even if the product data is otherwise complete.
SKU must be unique across all products in the catalog.
A DISCONTINUED product is in a terminal state and cannot be modified. Any attempt to update, add images, or change the status of a discontinued product must be rejected.
A DISCONTINUED product is in a terminal state and cannot be modified. Any attempt to update, add images, or change the status of a discontinued product must be rejected.

storage.notes: Product gallery images. Served publicly via CDN; the catalog BC uploads binaries and persists the returned public URL on each ProductImage.

outbound.rationale: Inventory creates a StockItem when a product is activated and permanently closes it when the product is discontinued. These are implicit lifecycle dependencies not visible in the checkout flow.

inbound.rationale:
Orders BC queries catalog at checkout to obtain the authoritative price snapshot for each product in the cart. Mandatory HTTP synchronous call for monetary integrity — prevents OWASP A04 fraud via stale or manipulated prices.
Mandatory HTTP for monetary snapshot (OrderLine.unitPrice reads Product.price). Designer evaluated LRM trade-offs including OWASP A04 fraud risk and chose HTTP. Price is always read from catalog in real time at checkout — no inconsistency window. The customer sends only productId + quantity; the backend reads the authoritative price.

integrations.contextMap:
flowchart LR
  n_catalog["catalog"]:::center
  n_inventory["inventory"]
  n_catalog -->|event / message-broker| n_inventory
  n_orders["orders"]
  n_orders -->|customer-supplier / http| n_catalog
  classDef center fill:#111827,color:#fff,stroke:#111827;
  classDef external fill:#fff7ed,stroke:#fb923c,color:#7c2d12;

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.
sagas.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant bc_orders as orders
  participant bc_inventory as inventory
  participant bc_payments as payments
  participant bc_delivery as delivery
  Note over bc_orders: trigger: OrderPlaced
  bc_orders->>bc_inventory: 1. OrderPlaced
  Note over bc_inventory: onFailure: StockReservationFailed / compensacion: StockReleased
  bc_inventory->>bc_payments: 2. StockReserved
  Note over bc_payments: onFailure: PaymentFailed / compensacion: PaymentRefunded
  bc_payments->>bc_orders: 3. PaymentApproved [UC-ORD-020 ConfirmOrder]
  bc_orders->>bc_delivery: 4. OrderConfirmed
  Note over bc_delivery: ✓ DeliveryOrderCreated

boundedContexts.name: orders
boundedContexts.purpose: Manages the full order lifecycle from cart creation through checkout, payment confirmation, delivery tracking and order completion. Validates prices against catalog via HTTP at checkout (monetary snapshot — OWASP A04). Maintains a local read model of customer delivery addresses fed by CustomerAddressUpdated events from the customers BC. Coordinator of the CheckoutSaga.

ruleDetails.description: A customer can only have one OPEN cart at a time. Before creating a new cart, the handler must verify that no OPEN cart already exists for this customer using cartRepository.findOpenByCustomerId(customerId).
A cart can only be modified (add, update, remove items) or checked out when its status is OPEN. CHECKED_OUT carts are immutable.
A cart can only be modified (add, update, remove items) or checked out when its status is OPEN. CHECKED_OUT carts are immutable.
A cart can only be modified (add, update, remove items) or checked out when its status is OPEN. CHECKED_OUT carts are immutable.
The cart must have at least one CartItem before it can be checked out. An empty cart cannot proceed to checkout.
A cart can only be modified (add, update, remove items) or checked out when its status is OPEN. CHECKED_OUT carts are immutable.
An order can only be cancelled when its status is PENDING_PAYMENT or CONFIRMED. IN_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, and CANCELLED orders cannot be cancelled.
A DELIVERED order is in a terminal state and cannot be transitioned or modified in any way.
A CANCELLED order is in a terminal state and cannot be transitioned or modified in any way.
An order can only be confirmed when its status is PENDING_PAYMENT. This guard ensures PaymentApproved events are idempotently processed.
An order can only be cancelled when its status is PENDING_PAYMENT or CONFIRMED. IN_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, and CANCELLED orders cannot be cancelled.
An order can only be cancelled when its status is PENDING_PAYMENT or CONFIRMED. IN_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, and CANCELLED orders cannot be cancelled.
An order can only be set to IN_DELIVERY when its status is CONFIRMED. This guard ensures DeliveryOrderCreated events are idempotently processed.
An order can only be completed (DELIVERED) when its status is IN_DELIVERY. This guard ensures DeliveryCompleted events are idempotently processed.

outbound.rationale:
Mandatory synchronous HTTP call to catalog at checkout time to obtain the authoritative price for each product in the cart. Mandatory HTTP for monetary integrity — prevents OWASP A04 fraud via stale or client-manipulated prices. The customer never sends prices; prices are always fetched from the catalog.
Mandatory HTTP for monetary snapshot (OrderLine.unitPrice reads Product.price). Designer evaluated LRM trade-offs including OWASP A04 fraud risk and chose HTTP. Price is always read from catalog in real time at checkout — no inconsistency window. The customer sends only productId + quantity; the backend reads the authoritative price.
OrderConfirmed triggers DeliveryOrder creation (step 4). OrderCancelled triggers delivery cancellation if the delivery was already created.
Triggers stock reservation. Saga trigger event (step 1).
Notifications BC sends an order confirmation message when the order is confirmed and a cancellation notice when the order is cancelled.

Payments BC consumes OrderCancelled to check if a card payment was already captured. If so, it initiates a refund via payment-gateway and emits PaymentRefunded. This closes the compensation chain for post-confirmation cancellations that occur outside the CheckoutSaga (e.g., admin cancellation, customer cancellation request).

inbound.rationale: Orders maintains a local read model of customer addresses fed by this event. Designer chose LRM for delivery address (non-monetary identity data). Eventual consistency accepted (typical lag < 1-2s). Step 2 must define the initial snapshot strategy (e.g., one-time HTTP call at first order placement before the LRM is warmed up).
DeliveryOrderCreated confirms to orders that the delivery is being arranged. DeliveryCompleted transitions the order to DELIVERED status.

StockReservationFailed triggers order cancellation (OrderCancelled) in orders. StockReleased is the compensation confirmation emitted by inventory after releasing reserved stock; orders receives it for saga state tracking.
PaymentApproved triggers order confirmation (step 3). PaymentFailed triggers order cancellation (compensation path). PaymentRefunded confirms the refund saga step for card payments.

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.
sagas.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant bc_orders as orders
  participant bc_inventory as inventory
  participant bc_payments as payments
  participant bc_delivery as delivery
  Note over bc_orders: trigger: OrderPlaced
  bc_orders->>bc_inventory: 1. OrderPlaced
  Note over bc_inventory: onFailure: StockReservationFailed / compensacion: StockReleased
  bc_inventory->>bc_payments: 2. StockReserved
  Note over bc_payments: onFailure: PaymentFailed / compensacion: PaymentRefunded
  bc_payments->>bc_orders: 3. PaymentApproved [UC-ORD-020 ConfirmOrder]
  bc_orders->>bc_delivery: 4. OrderConfirmed
  Note over bc_delivery: ✓ DeliveryOrderCreated

boundedContexts.name: payments
boundedContexts.purpose: Processes and records payments for orders. Supports card payments via an external payment gateway and cash-on-delivery payments recorded when the courier collects the cash. Handles refunds for cancelled confirmed orders charged by card.

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.

boundedContexts.name: inventory
boundedContexts.purpose: Tracks real-time stock levels for each active product. Creates a StockItem when a product is activated and permanently closes it when the product is discontinued. Reserves stock during the CheckoutSaga and releases it as compensation if payment fails.

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.

  bc_inventory->>bc_payments: 2. StockReserved
  Note over bc_payments: onFailure: PaymentFailed / compensacion: PaymentRefunded
  bc_payments->>bc_orders: 3. PaymentApproved [UC-ORD-020 ConfirmOrder]
  bc_orders->>bc_delivery: 4. OrderConfirmed
  Note over bc_delivery: ✓ DeliveryOrderCreated

orders consumes event "StockReleased" from "inventory" which is declared in system.yaml but not yet designed (no inventory.yaml in arch/).

boundedContexts.name: delivery
boundedContexts.purpose: Manages home delivery orders using the company's own courier fleet. Handles the full delivery lifecycle from assignment to completion. Records cash-on-delivery events for the payments BC.

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.

boundedContexts.name: customers
boundedContexts.purpose: Manages customer accounts and their delivery addresses. Publishes CustomerAddressUpdated events consumed by orders to maintain a local read model of delivery addresses for order snapshots.

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.
sagas.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant bc_orders as orders
  participant bc_inventory as inventory
  participant bc_payments as payments
  participant bc_delivery as delivery
  Note over bc_orders: trigger: OrderPlaced
  bc_orders->>bc_inventory: 1. OrderPlaced
  Note over bc_inventory: onFailure: StockReservationFailed / compensacion: StockReleased
  bc_inventory->>bc_payments: 2. StockReserved
  Note over bc_payments: onFailure: PaymentFailed / compensacion: PaymentRefunded
  bc_payments->>bc_orders: 3. PaymentApproved [UC-ORD-020 ConfirmOrder]
  bc_orders->>bc_delivery: 4. OrderConfirmed
  Note over bc_delivery: ✓ DeliveryOrderCreated

diagnostics.message: orders consumes event "CustomerAddressUpdated" from "customers" which is declared in system.yaml but not yet designed (no customers.yaml in arch/).

boundedContexts.name: notifications
boundedContexts.purpose: Delivers transactional notifications to customers (email, SMS, push) for key order lifecycle events: order confirmed, order cancelled, delivery completed. Fully delegates sending to an external notification provider. Generic domain — no competitive advantage; no business logic beyond template selection and dispatch.

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.
sagas.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant bc_orders as orders
  participant bc_inventory as inventory
  participant bc_payments as payments
  participant bc_delivery as delivery
  Note over bc_orders: trigger: OrderPlaced
  bc_orders->>bc_inventory: 1. OrderPlaced
  Note over bc_inventory: onFailure: StockReservationFailed / compensacion: StockReleased
  bc_inventory->>bc_payments: 2. StockReserved
  Note over bc_payments: onFailure: PaymentFailed / compensacion: PaymentRefunded
  bc_payments->>bc_orders: 3. PaymentApproved [UC-ORD-020 ConfirmOrder]
  bc_orders->>bc_delivery: 4. OrderConfirmed
  Note over bc_delivery: ✓ DeliveryOrderCreated

sagas.name: CheckoutSaga
sagas.description: Coordinates the checkout process across inventory, payments, orders and delivery. Reserves stock, processes payment, confirms the order and triggers home delivery. Compensates by releasing reserved stock and refunding the card charge if any step fails.
sagas.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant bc_orders as orders
  participant bc_inventory as inventory
  participant bc_payments as payments
  participant bc_delivery as delivery
  Note over bc_orders: trigger: OrderPlaced
  bc_orders->>bc_inventory: 1. OrderPlaced
  Note over bc_inventory: onFailure: StockReservationFailed / compensacion: StockReleased
  bc_inventory->>bc_payments: 2. StockReserved
  Note over bc_payments: onFailure: PaymentFailed / compensacion: PaymentRefunded
  bc_payments->>bc_orders: 3. PaymentApproved [UC-ORD-020 ConfirmOrder]
  bc_orders->>bc_delivery: 4. OrderConfirmed
  Note over bc_delivery: ✓ DeliveryOrderCreated
